sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés, currently sitting at 17th in the league with 35 points, host Valencia, who are positioned at 10th with 45 points, at the Estadio de Mendizorroza in Vitoria-Gasteiz. This matchup promises to be intriguing as Alavés fight to climb out of the relegation zone, while Valencia aim to solidify their mid-table standing. The referee, Jesus Gil Manzano, is known for showing an average of 5.1 yellow cards per game, which could influence the physicality of this encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Valencia have dominated with 3 wins compared to Alavés’ 1 win and 1 draw. Their most recent clash on December 22, 2024, ended in a thrilling 2-2 draw, with Luis Rioja earning the Player of the Match award for Valencia with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8.3. Head-to-head streaks show Alavés have avoided defeat in their last 4 home games against Valencia, while both teams have seen less than 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Deportivo Alavés
sections.text: For Alavés, forward Kike García stands out with 12 goals this season, significantly overperforming his expected goals (xG) of 8.43 by 3.57. Midfielder Joan Jordán also contributes with a solid average rating of 7.07, having scored 4 goals and provided 2 assists. Another name to note is defender Nahuel Tenaglia, who boasts the highest average rating in the squad at 7.09, while contributing 2 goals and 1 assist. However, they’ll miss key defender Abdelkabir Abqar, who ranks 5th in the league for percentage of long balls in total passes at 9%.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Valencia
sections.text: Valencia’s attack is led by forward Hugo Duro, who has netted 11 goals, overperforming his xG of 9.37 by 1.63. Midfielder Diego López is another danger man with 8 goals and 5 assists, while Luis Rioja, a standout in their last meeting, has 5 goals and 3 assists with a rating of 6.96. Defender Yarek Gasiorowski also catches the eye with a league rank of 4 for ground duels won at 71%. However, they’ll be without midfielder Javier Guerra, who has contributed 3 goals and 3 assists this season.

sections.subtitle: Standout Player in Recent Meetings
sections.text: Umar Sadiq of Valencia has been a consistent performer against Alavés in their last 5 matchups, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.4. Despite only starting 2 of those games, he has scored 2 crucial goals, though he hasn’t recorded any assists. His presence on the pitch, with 5 goals this season for Valencia, will be a concern for the Alavés defense.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Statistics - Deportivo Alavés
sections.text: Alavés have struggled offensively, scoring 35 goals this season, ranking 15th in the league, while conceding 47 goals, placing them 10th for least goals conceded. They excel in specific areas, ranking 1st for tackles per game at 19.43 and 2nd for penalty area entries per game at 30.43. Their conversion rate of big chances is impressive at 44.44%, ranking 2nd in the league, though they’ve missed 30 big chances, the fewest in the league. Goal distribution shows they score most late in games, with 29.79% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Statistics - Valencia
sections.text: Valencia have shown resilience with a streak of 10 games without a loss and often scoring first in 8 of their last 10 matches. Their goal distribution indicates strength in the 31st to 45th-minute period, where they’ve scored 29.41% of their goals. They’ve been involved in low-scoring games recently against Alavés, aligning with the head-to-head trend of under 2.5 goals. Their defensive structure will be tested, as they’ve failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 away games against Alavés.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Discipline
sections.text: Alavés rank 2nd for fouls committed with 568 this season and 18th for least yellow cards with 96, averaging 2.74 per game, which could lead to a card-heavy game under referee Jesus Gil Manzano. Their streak of more than 4.5 cards in 7 of their last 9 home games supports this expectation. Both teams historically see less than 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 head-to-heads, but Alavés’ aggressive style, ranking 1st for tackles per game, might tip the balance. Corner stats show Alavés averaging 4.54 per game (12th in the league), while head-to-head trends indicate less than 10.5 corners in 7 of their last 9 meetings.
